    mondrian_tree_nodes ¶

    Classes:

    • MondrianBranchClassifier –

      Mondrian Tree Classifier branch node.

    • MondrianBranchRegressor –

      Mondrian Tree Regressor branch node.

    • MondrianLeaf –

      Prototype class for all types of nodes in a Mondrian Tree.

    • MondrianLeafClassifier –

      Mondrian Tree Classifier leaf node.

    • MondrianLeafRegressor –

      Mondrian Tree Regressor leaf node.

    • MondrianNode –

      Representation of a node within a Mondrian tree

    • MondrianNodeClassifier –
    • MondrianNodeRegressor –

    MondrianBranchClassifier ¶

    MondrianBranchClassifier(
    parent, time, depth, feature, threshold, *children
)

    Bases: MondrianNodeClassifier, MondrianBranch

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchClassifier[MondrianBranchClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier[MondrianNodeClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode[MondrianNode]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch[MondrianBranch]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchClassifier
                                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier
                

                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchClassifier
                


              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch"

    Mondrian Tree Classifier branch node.

    Parameters¶

    parent Parent node of the branch. time Split time characterizing the branch. depth Depth of the branch in the tree. feature Feature of the branch. threshold Acceptation threshold of the branch. *children Children nodes of the branch.

    Methods:

    • is_dirac –

      Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given

    • loss –

      Compute the loss of the node.

    • predict –

      Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • replant –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    • score –

      Compute the score of the node.

    • update_count –

      Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    is_dirac ¶

    is_dirac(y: ClfTarget) -> bool

    Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given class, i.e., if the node is pure regarding the given class.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    loss ¶

    loss(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the loss of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y A given class of the problem. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the problem. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    predict ¶

    predict(
    dirichlet: float, classes: set, n_classes: int
) -> dict[ClfTarget, float]

    Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary with the new values.

    Parameters¶

    dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. classes The set of classes seen so far n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    replant ¶

    replant(
    leaf: MondrianNodeClassifier, copy_all: bool = False
)

    Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    score ¶

    score(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the score of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y Class for which we want the score. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes seen so far.

    Notes¶

    This uses Jeffreys prior with Dirichlet parameter for smoothing.

    update_count ¶

    update_count(y)

    Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node (not to use twice if you add one sample).

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_downwards ¶

    update_downwards(
    x,
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    do_update_weight: bool,
    n_classes: int,
)

    Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to proceed. y Class of the sample x. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Should it use the aggregation or not step Step of the tree. do_update_weight Should we update the weights of the node as well. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight ¶

    update_weight(
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    n_classes: int,
) -> float

    Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Whether to use aggregation or not during computation (given by the tree). step Step parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianBranchRegressor ¶

    MondrianBranchRegressor(
    parent, time, depth, feature, threshold, *children
)

    Bases: MondrianNodeRegressor, MondrianBranch

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchRegressor[MondrianBranchRegressor]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeRegressor[MondrianNodeRegressor]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode[MondrianNode]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch[MondrianBranch]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeRegressor --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchRegressor
                                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeRegressor
                

                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchRegressor
                


              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchRegressor href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranchRegressor"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeRegressor href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeRegressor"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianBranch"

    Mondrian Tree Regressor branch node.

    Parameters¶

    parent Parent node of the branch. time Split time characterizing the branch. depth Depth of the branch in the tree. feature Feature of the branch. threshold Acceptation threshold of the branch. *children Children nodes of the branch.

    Methods:

    • loss –

      Compute the loss of the node.

    • predict –

      Return the prediction of the node.

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • replant –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a label and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    loss ¶

    loss(sample_value: RegTarget) -> float

    Compute the loss of the node.

    Parameters¶

    sample_value A given value.

    predict ¶

    predict() -> RegTarget

    Return the prediction of the node.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    replant ¶

    replant(
    leaf: MondrianNodeRegressor, copy_all: bool = False
)

    Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_downwards ¶

    update_downwards(
    x,
    sample_value: RegTarget,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    do_update_weight: bool,
)

    Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to proceed (as a list). sample_value Label of the sample x. use_aggregation Should it use the aggregation or not step Step of the tree. do_update_weight Should we update the weights of the node as well.

    update_weight ¶

    update_weight(
    sample_value: RegTarget,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
) -> float

    Update the weight of the node given a label and the method used.

    Parameters¶

    sample_value Label of a given sample. use_aggregation Whether to use aggregation or not during computation (given by the tree). step Step parameter of the tree.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianLeaf ¶

    MondrianLeaf(parent, time, depth)

    Bases: Leaf

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf[MondrianLeaf]

              

              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf"

    Prototype class for all types of nodes in a Mondrian Tree.

    Parameters¶

    parent Parent Node. time Split time of the node for Mondrian process. depth Depth of the leaf.

    MondrianLeafClassifier ¶

    MondrianLeafClassifier(parent, time, depth)

    Bases: MondrianNodeClassifier, MondrianLeaf

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafClassifier[MondrianLeafClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier[MondrianNodeClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode[MondrianNode]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf[MondrianLeaf]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafClassifier
                                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier
                

                vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafClassifier
                


              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf"

    Mondrian Tree Classifier leaf node.

    Parameters¶

    parent Parent node. time Split time of the node. depth The depth of the leaf.

    Methods:

    • is_dirac –

      Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given

    • loss –

      Compute the loss of the node.

    • predict –

      Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • replant –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    • score –

      Compute the score of the node.

    • update_count –

      Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    is_dirac ¶

    is_dirac(y: ClfTarget) -> bool

    Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given class, i.e., if the node is pure regarding the given class.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    loss ¶

    loss(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the loss of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y A given class of the problem. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the problem. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    predict ¶

    predict(
    dirichlet: float, classes: set, n_classes: int
) -> dict[ClfTarget, float]

    Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary with the new values.

    Parameters¶

    dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. classes The set of classes seen so far n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    replant ¶

    replant(
    leaf: MondrianNodeClassifier, copy_all: bool = False
)

    Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    score ¶

    score(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the score of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y Class for which we want the score. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes seen so far.

    Notes¶

    This uses Jeffreys prior with Dirichlet parameter for smoothing.

    update_count ¶

    update_count(y)

    Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node (not to use twice if you add one sample).

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_downwards ¶

    update_downwards(
    x,
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    do_update_weight: bool,
    n_classes: int,
)

    Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to proceed. y Class of the sample x. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Should it use the aggregation or not step Step of the tree. do_update_weight Should we update the weights of the node as well. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight ¶

    update_weight(
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    n_classes: int,
) -> float

    Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Whether to use aggregation or not during computation (given by the tree). step Step parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianLeafRegressor ¶

    MondrianLeafRegressor(parent, time, depth)

    Bases: MondrianNodeRegressor, MondrianLeaf

              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeafRegressor[MondrianLeafRegressor]

              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianLeaf"

    Mondrian Tree Regressor leaf node.

    Parameters¶

    parent Parent node. time Split time of the node. depth The depth of the leaf.

    Methods:

    • loss –

      Compute the loss of the node.

    • predict –

      Return the prediction of the node.

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • replant –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a label and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    loss ¶

    loss(sample_value: RegTarget) -> float

    Compute the loss of the node.

    Parameters¶

    sample_value A given value.

    predict ¶

    predict() -> RegTarget

    Return the prediction of the node.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    replant ¶

    replant(
    leaf: MondrianNodeRegressor, copy_all: bool = False
)

    Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_downwards ¶

    update_downwards(
    x,
    sample_value: RegTarget,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    do_update_weight: bool,
)

    Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to proceed (as a list). sample_value Label of the sample x. use_aggregation Should it use the aggregation or not step Step of the tree. do_update_weight Should we update the weights of the node as well.

    update_weight ¶

    update_weight(
    sample_value: RegTarget,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
) -> float

    Update the weight of the node given a label and the method used.

    Parameters¶

    sample_value Label of a given sample. use_aggregation Whether to use aggregation or not during computation (given by the tree). step Step parameter of the tree.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianNode ¶

    MondrianNode(*args, **kwargs)

    Bases: Base

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode[MondrianNode]

              

              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"

    Representation of a node within a Mondrian tree

    Methods:

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianNodeClassifier ¶

    MondrianNodeClassifier(*args, **kwargs)

    Bases: MondrianNode

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier[MondrianNodeClassifier]
              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode[MondrianNode]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode --> vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier
                


              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNodeClassifier"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.mondrian.mondrian_tree_nodes.MondrianNode"

    Methods:

    • is_dirac –

      Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given

    • loss –

      Compute the loss of the node.

    • predict –

      Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary

    • range –

      Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    • range_extension –

      Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    • replant –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    • score –

      Compute the score of the node.

    • update_count –

      Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node

    • update_depth –

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    is_dirac ¶

    is_dirac(y: ClfTarget) -> bool

    Check whether the node follows a dirac distribution regarding the given class, i.e., if the node is pure regarding the given class.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    loss ¶

    loss(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the loss of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y A given class of the problem. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the problem. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    predict ¶

    predict(
    dirichlet: float, classes: set, n_classes: int
) -> dict[ClfTarget, float]

    Predict the scores of all classes and output a scores dictionary with the new values.

    Parameters¶

    dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. classes The set of classes seen so far n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    range ¶

    range(feature) -> tuple[float, float]

    Output the known range of the node regarding the j-th feature.

    Parameters¶

    feature Feature for which you want to know the range.

    range_extension ¶

    range_extension(x) -> tuple[float, dict[ClfTarget, float]]

    Compute the range extension of the node for the given sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to deal with.

    replant ¶

    replant(
    leaf: MondrianNodeClassifier, copy_all: bool = False
)

    Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

    score ¶

    score(
    y: ClfTarget, dirichlet: float, n_classes: int
) -> float

    Compute the score of the node.

    Parameters¶

    y Class for which we want the score. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes seen so far.

    Notes¶

    This uses Jeffreys prior with Dirichlet parameter for smoothing.

    update_count ¶

    update_count(y)

    Update the amount of samples that belong to a class in the node (not to use twice if you add one sample).

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample.

    update_depth ¶

    update_depth(depth)

    Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    Parameters¶

    depth Depth of the node.

    update_downwards ¶

    update_downwards(
    x,
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    do_update_weight: bool,
    n_classes: int,
)

    Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample to proceed. y Class of the sample x. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Should it use the aggregation or not step Step of the tree. do_update_weight Should we update the weights of the node as well. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight ¶

    update_weight(
    y: ClfTarget,
    dirichlet: float,
    use_aggregation: bool,
    step: float,
    n_classes: int,
) -> float

    Update the weight of the node given a class and the method used.

    Parameters¶

    y Class of a given sample. dirichlet Dirichlet parameter of the tree. use_aggregation Whether to use aggregation or not during computation (given by the tree). step Step parameter of the tree. n_classes The total number of classes of the problem.

    update_weight_tree ¶

    update_weight_tree()

    Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    MondrianNodeRegressor ¶

    MondrianNodeRegressor(*args, **kwargs)

    Bases: MondrianNode

    • loss –

      Transfer information from a leaf to a new branch.

      Update the depth of the current node with the given depth.

    • update_downwards –

      Update the node when running a downward procedure updating the tree.

    • update_weight –

      Update the weight of the node given a label and the method used.

    • update_weight_tree –

      Update the weight of the node in the tree.

    sample_value: RegTarget,
